mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment (UNRATE above 6%) signals substantial labor market slack, which depresses wage growth and inflationary pressure, causing the 10-year yield to decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or a decrease in UNRATE does not consistently precede yield declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 950,000 units signals strengthening residential investment and broader economic demand, leading to higher long-term yields as growth expectations rise over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 950, the 10-year yield falls in more than 60% of subsequent 60-day windows, or the signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate a contraction in real activity, heightening recession fears and driving a flight to safety that reduces the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: When both series decline, the 10-year yield rises in more than 50% of cases, or the joint signal does not exhibit contingency table p < 0.05 over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPI YoY near or below 2%), any upward tick in CPIAUCSL breaches the credibility of central bank dovishness, prompting expectations of eventual policy normalization and pushing the 10-year yield higher over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: In periods with CPI YoY below 2%, a rise in CPIAUCSL fails to correlate with positive 20-day yield changes (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate from its exceptionally low level (0.08%) signals the onset of monetary tightening, which lifts term premiums and causes the 10-year yield to rise sharply over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.10%, the 10-year yield declines in more than 50% of subsequent 5-day windows, or the average yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirm robust domestic demand, amplifying growth expectations and pushing the 10-year yield higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: When both series rise, the 10-year yield falls in more than 40% of cases, or the joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession period with high unemployment (UNRATE > 5%) and ultra-low rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%), a meaningful decline in UNRATE signals a durable recovery, triggering a rise in the 10-year yield as growth and inflation expectations improve over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession high unemployment with accommodative policy
failure_condition: When UNRATE declines more than 0.1% in the specified regime, the 10-year yield does not rise more than 50% of the time, or the average yield change is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly reading in real GDP (GDPC1) above its recent average validates the strength of the underlying expansion, leading to higher term premiums and a gradual rise in the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 5-year walk-forward, the Rank IC of GDPC1 on 60-day yield changes remains below 0.02, or positive GDP surprises are followed by yield declines in more than 50% of instances.
